# Iskander Helm Charts

Umbrella Helm chart for deploying Iskander on K3s (or any Kubernetes distribution).

## Quick start

```bash
# Add Bitnami repo (used for PostgreSQL and Redis subcharts)
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

# Fetch subchart dependencies
helm dependency update infra/helm/iskander

# Dry-run to verify templates render correctly
helm template iskander infra/helm/iskander \
  --values infra/helm/iskander/values-standard.yaml

# Install (passwords are generated by the install script in production)
helm install iskander infra/helm/iskander \
  --namespace iskander \
  --create-namespace \
  --values infra/helm/iskander/values-standard.yaml \
  --set global.postgresql.auth.postgresPassword=$(openssl rand -base64 24) \
  --set global.postgresql.auth.password=$(openssl rand -base64 24) \
  --set global.redis.auth.password=$(openssl rand -base64 24)
```

## Hardware profiles

| Profile | RAM | Use case |
|---------|-----|---------|
| `values-minimal.yaml` | 8 GB | Testing, resource-constrained |
| `values-standard.yaml` | 16 GB | Production (recommended) |

## Directory structure

```
infra/helm/iskander/
├── Chart.yaml           # Umbrella chart + dependency declarations
├── values.yaml          # Base values (all services disabled by default)
├── values-minimal.yaml  # 8 GB resource profile
├── values-standard.yaml # 16 GB resource profile
├── files/
│   └── init.sql         # Idempotent per-service DB + role provisioning
└── templates/
    ├── _helpers.tpl                    # Shared template helpers
    ├── namespace.yaml                  # iskander namespace
    ├── resourcequota.yaml              # Namespace resource quota
    ├── configmap-initdb.yaml           # Mounts init.sql into PostgreSQL initdb
    ├── networkpolicy-default-deny.yaml # Default-deny all + allow DNS
    ├── networkpolicy-postgresql.yaml   # Allow pods labelled db-access: postgresql
    └── networkpolicy-redis.yaml        # Allow pods labelled db-access: redis
```

## Network policy model

All ingress and egress is denied by default. Services opt in by:

1. Setting the label `iskander.coop/db-access: postgresql` (or `redis`) on their pods
2. Adding an `egress` rule in their own NetworkPolicy to reach the database

This means a compromised service cannot reach other services' databases.

## Adding a new service

1. Create a Helm chart (or wrapper values) in `infra/helm/<service>/`
2. Add it as a `condition`-gated dependency in `Chart.yaml`
3. Add its database/role to `files/init.sql`
4. Add its network policy to `templates/networkpolicy-<service>.yaml`
5. Add a toggle to `values.yaml` with `enabled: false`

## Minimum requirements

- K3s v1.28+ (or any Kubernetes 1.25+)
- 8 GB RAM (minimal profile), 16 GB RAM (standard profile)
- 64 GB SSD recommended
- ARM64 or x86_64
